summary: Opus 3-tier catalyst sweep (chunk 8 (FRHC-GTY)): scan -> deep -> skeptic

survivors_flagged.one_line: Announced May 4 2026 go-private at $9.50/share; definitive deal pending close (multiple fairness probes). Live merger-arb spread.
Definitive split-up merger-arb: Smith Ventures buys fintech, CommerceOne buys the bank; Q2 2026 close; holders get $8.11 cash + 0.2215 NewCo-bank shares (live arb spread + stub).

DayOne (its spun-out Singapore DC co, GDS retains minority stake) filing $5B Nasdaq+Singapore dual-listing IPO at ~$20B in 2026; sum-of-the-parts value crystallization plus $385M share repurchase from GDS.
Policy-forced detention buildout: ICE warehouse-to-detention drive + 6,000 idle beds being filled toward '100,000+' capacity; FY26 guide $2.9-3.1B (dated structural ramp, partly policy-macro).
IMpactMF interim overall-survival analysis in relapsed/refractory myelofibrosis expected 2H 2026 — dated binary with asymmetry layered on approved Rytelo.
GFORCE-1 (MRT-8102, 85% hsCRP cut) cardiac/CRP readout dated H2 2026; Novartis collab (up to $2.1B + 30% US profit) adds optionality. One real dated catalyst -> watch.
Tungsten (Pilot Mountain) critical-minerals story; DPA Title-III-funded PFS with Plan-of-Operations submission targeted Aug 2026 — real dated milestone but soft (permitting step, not a hard re-rate).

Live hostile all-cash tender from Diana Shipping at $24.80 (board rejected 3x, latest Jun 2); proxy fight with annual meeting Jun 18 and tender expiry Jun 26 — two imminent hard dated catalysts, stock sits right at the bid.

Genuine Parts announced a definitive tax-free separation into two public cos (Global Automotive + Global Industrial) alongside an Elliott cooperation agreement; real spin + activist+structural, but completion targeted Q1 2027 (not imminent).

Structure Therapeutics oral GLP-1 (aleniglipron): dated forward reads — ACCESS OLE Q3'26, Body-Comp/T2D Q4'26, Phase-3 initiation Q3'26 — but the headline efficacy already printed positive (16.3% WL) in Mar'26, so the biggest asymmetry is partly re-rated.

GeoPark: definitive Frontera Colombia E&P acquisition ($375M, Jan'26) in progress + Grupo Gilinski strategic anchor stake (Mar'26) + dead Parex $9 bid history (13D/A on file); real structural change, though the takeover binary is gone and the deal is partly fired.

Amazon definitive merger at $90/sh cash-or-0.3210 AMZN (capped); majority written-consent done, no vote needed, close expected 2027 pending regulatory — defined-price arb, long-dated, up-to-$110M milestone clawback.

Silicon-metal trade-case milestones: DOC final AD rates for Australia/Norway late-June 2026 + US ITC final decision late-July 2026 — dated rulings that re-rate pricing power; ~70-80% of book already protected.

Live merger-arb: Baker Hughes $13.6B all-cash (replaced terminated Flowserve MOE); Chart holders approved Oct-2025, EU Phase I review underway (Form CO 5/21), close expected July 2026 — dated, multi-gate, suppressed underlying.

verified.catalyst: Long Lake Management (backed by General Catalyst + Alpha Wave) is taking Amex GBT private at $9.50/share all-cash in a $6.3B definitive merger with no financing condition and 69% of shares locked via voting agreements; closing pending stockholder vote + antitrust clearance.
verified.dated_milestone: HSR filed ~May 22 2026; special-meeting stockholder vote pending; expected close 2H 2026; outside date Nov 2 2026 (extendable to Feb 2 2027). Next: virtual special meeting / proxy effectiveness.
verified.instrument: Common stock (merger-arb long): buy ~$9.34, deliver into $9.50 cash at close = ~$0.16 / ~1.7% gross spread.
verified.bloom_gates: G1 (named counterparty: Long Lake Management / General Catalyst / Alpha Wave) + G2 (concrete commitment: signed definitive merger agreement, $9.50 cash, no financing condition, 69% voting-agreement support).
verified.edge: L-M — spread is thin (~1.7% gross) reflecting high deal certainty (unanimous board, 69% locked holders, all-cash, no financing condition); low-risk/low-return arb, not a mispricing. Upside annualizes acceptably if close lands by ~Nov, but downside is the regulatory-delay drag to Feb 2027 outside date.
verified.primary_source: Businesswire press release, "Long Lake Agrees to Acquire American Express Global Business Travel ... for $6.3 Billion," May 4 2026; GBTG PREM14A merger proxy (HSR filed ~May 22 2026; outside date Nov 2 2026 / Feb 2 2027); FMP quote $9.34 (2026-06-06).
verified.kill_fact: All load-bearing facts confirmed vs primary sources. Merger terms verified across BusinessWire (announce 2026-05-04), Amex GBT press release, and Latham & Watkins: Long Lake Management (backed by General Catalyst + Alpha Wave) acquiring Amex GBT at $9.50/share all-cash, ~$6.3B, NO financing condition, 69% of shares locked via voting agreements (Amex, Expedia, QIA, BlackRock); close targeted H2 2026 pending stockholder vote + antitrust. Live FMP quote = $9.34 (NYSE, prevClose 9.35, dayHigh 9.38, vol 1.1M) vs $9.50 cash = $0.16 / ~1.7% gross spread — entry price and spread match the line exactly. Forward correctly framed: announcement fired but the cash-settlement CLOSE is the pending catalyst. The 'fairness probes' (Kaskela, Schall, Halper Sadeh, Monteverde, KSF, Brodsky & Smith) are confirmed routine plaintiff-firm investigations, not a credible block. Edge is genuine, idiosyncratic, and tradeable but thin (~1.7% gross to an H2-2026 close), justifying a mid score. Minor: 'HSR filed ~May 22 / outside date Nov 2 2026' not independently re-verified but immaterial to the arb thesis.
verified.analysis: Real, specific, dated, forward merger-arb: the May 4 2026 announcement fired, but the deal CLOSE — the cash-settlement event driving the residual $9.34 vs $9.50 spread — remains pending the stockholder vote and HSR/foreign antitrust clearance (filed ~May 22 2026; outside date Nov 2 2026). Deal certainty is high (definitive agreement, no financing condition, 69% of shares signed to voting agreements incl. Amex/Expedia/QIA/BlackRock, unanimous board+special committee), and the "fairness probes" flagged in triage are routine plaintiff-firm investigations rather than a credible block. Gating ACTIVE but scoring 6: the catalyst is genuine and idiosyncratic (not macro), yet the edge is modest because the market has already priced near deal value, leaving a tight, high-confidence/low-return spread.

verified.catalyst: Definitive 2-step split-up: CommerceOne Financial acquires Green Dot's bank (holders get $8.11 cash + 0.2215 shares of a new public bank holding co) while Smith Ventures LLC buys the fintech/embedded-finance unit for $690M cash; Q2 2026 close.
verified.dated_milestone: Shareholder vote on the deal scheduled June 23, 2026 (per definitive proxy filed May 8, 2026); expected close Q2 2026.
verified.bloom_gates: G1+G2+G3 (G1 named counterparties Smith Ventures LLC & CommerceOne Financial; G2 signed definitive agreements w/ $690M cash + $8.11/0.2215 exchange ratio; G3 unpriced 0.2215 NewCo-bank stub + live arb spread vs implied $14.25-$19.18 range)
verified.edge: M - deal is real, named, dated and the NewCo bank-share stub is genuinely unpriced, but the spread (price $12.66 vs $8.11 cash + 0.2215 illiquid NewCo shares) reflects real stub-valuation risk and active fairness/shareholder litigation (multiple firms investigating), capping conviction.
verified.primary_source: Wohl & Fruchter LLP / GlobeNewswire, "GDOT Alert" (May 13, 2026) detailing $8.11 cash + 0.2215 NewCo-bank shares, $690M Smith Ventures fintech sale, definitive proxy filed May 8 2026, vote June 23 2026; corroborated by Green Dot/BusinessWire deal announcement (Nov 23, 2025).
verified.kill_fact: Primary sources confirm the thesis: Nov 23/24 2025 8-K (Form 425) and DEFM14A proxy state each GDOT share converts to $8.11 cash + 0.2215 shares of New CommerceOne bank holding co, with Smith Ventures buying the non-bank fintech for $690M cash; special-meeting vote set June 23, 2026, close expected Q2 2026. Real-time quote shows GDOT $12.66 (timestamp 2026-06-06), well below the sponsor's $14.25-$19.18 stated range — forward/dated, NOT fired. Math holds: $12.66 - $8.11 locked cash = $4.55 = market's value of the 0.2215 unlisted NewCo stub. Fairness investigations confirmed (Wohl & Fruchter; Kaskela; multiple firms). CORRECTION: line claims 'definitive proxy filed May 8 2026' and 'Wohl & Fruchter alert May 13 2026' — the DEFM14A is a FY2026 filing and deal was announced Nov 2025 (not May); exact proxy-file date unverified and Kahn Swick & Foti alert not independently surfaced, but these are immaterial to the catalyst. Real edge = merger-arb with hard-to-value unlisted stub + bank-holding-co regulatory approval as the gating risk; score 7 appropriate.
verified.analysis: Confirmed via Wohl & Fruchter alert (May 13, 2026) and Kahn Swick & Foti / Kaskela alerts citing the exact $8.11 + 0.2215-share terms, $690M Smith Ventures fintech purchase, definitive proxy filed May 8 2026, and June 23 2026 vote. GDOT trades $12.66, below the sponsor's implied $14.25-$19.18 range; $8.11 is locked cash and the remaining ~$4.55 is the market's value of the 0.2215 unlisted NewCo bank shares, so the catalyst is a forward, dated merger-arb with a real stub option, not yet fired. Main risks are stub illiquidity/valuation and the cluster of fairness investigations that could force a price bump or, less likely, a vote hiccup.

verified.catalyst: GDS's ~30% stake in spun-out DayOne Data Centers (Singapore hyperscaler) is set to be marked to a ~$20B IPO valuation via a planned ~$5B SGX-Nasdaq dual listing, crystallizing sum-of-the-parts value well above GDS's current implied carrying mark.
verified.dated_milestone: DayOne confidential US IPO draft (F-1) reportedly "near"/imminent as of Bloomberg Mar 16 2026, with listing targeted before YE2026; next hard date = public F-1 filing / SGX GLB prospectus (not yet on file as of Jun 6 2026).
verified.bloom_gates: G3 (specific currently-unpriced figure: GDS's residual DayOne stake valued >US$2.2B at Series C price per GDS Jan-2026 disclosure, with IPO implying ~$20B co value / ~$5.6B+ stake — vs GDS equity that does not fully reflect it); partial G1 (named counterparty DayOne + underwriters JPM/MS/BofA/Citi).
verified.edge: M — value-crystallization is real and named, but IPO timing is soft (still "considering/near-filing," no public F-1, no priced deal, no fixed date), and the SOTP DayOne thesis is partly known/priced into GDS already.
verified.primary_source: GDS Holdings press release "GDS Announces Sale Of US$385 million DayOne Shares," Jan 13 2026 (SEC 6-K / gdsholdingsltd.gcs-web.com); DayOne IPO timing per Bloomberg "DayOne Data Centers Nears Confidential US IPO Filing," Mar 16 2026.
verified.analysis: The discrete, dated leg of the flag — GDS's US$385M DayOne share buyback — is already FIRED (definitive agreements Jan 13 2026, completed in Q1 2026, recycling ~95% of principal at ~6.5x MoM), so it is backward-looking and re-rated. The forward leg is DayOne's planned ~$5B dual SGX-Nasdaq IPO at ~$20B, which would mark GDS's remaining >US$2.2B stake to a public price (G3 unpriced-value); this is real and specific but timing remains "no final decision," with only a "near" confidential filing reported (Bloomberg, Mar 16 2026) and no public F-1 or pricing yet. Net: a genuine forward spin-off value-crystallization catalyst but event-date uncertain and partially anticipated, so WATCH rather than ACTIVE.

verified.catalyst: GEO Group's ICE detention buildout (warehouse-to-detention drive, ~6,000 idle high-security beds toward the administration's 100,000-bed goal, FY26 revenue guide $2.95-3.10B) — but the thesis is already issued and re-rated.
verified.dated_milestone: No specific forward dated milestone. Next scheduled event is Q2 2026 earnings (~early Aug 2026); company already guided Q2 rev $715-725M / EPS $0.25-0.29, so it is pre-priced, not a surprise catalyst.
verified.instrument: equity / long call (deep-ITM, but post-re-rating risk/reward poor)
verified.bloom_gates: None met cleanly. G1: counterparty (ICE) is named but the future buildout has no signed/dated contract — only optional capacity "if demand increases." G2: no concrete unconsummated commitment. G3: the $2.95-3.10B FY26 figure and $300M+ idle-bed upside are already disclosed and priced, not currently-unpriced.
verified.edge: L — thesis is consensus and fired; stock +25% in a single session (May 6, 2026) on the Q1 beat + guidance raise, now $25.31 vs 52wk low $12.51 (2x), 50d avg $19.89 / 200d $17.94. Remaining upside is a policy-macro bet (Trump 100k-bed agenda), which the gate excludes.
verified.primary_source: Benzinga, "Why Is...Geo Group Stock Soaring Today?" (May 6, 2026, +25.11% on Q1 beat + raised FY26 guide $2.95-3.10B); corroborated by StockStory/Investing.com Q1 2026 deep-dive (idle ~6,000 beds "if demand increases," D. Ray James 1,868-bed activation ~$66M); FMP quote 2026-06-06 (GEO $25.31, 52wk 12.51–27.90).
verified.analysis: GEO already re-rated +25.11% on May 6, 2026 when it beat Q1 (rev $705.2M, +17%), reactivated three idle facilities, and RAISED FY26 guidance to $2.95-3.10B / EPS $1.15-1.25 — so the named catalyst is fired and priced, not forward. The ~6,000 idle beds (~$300M+ potential annual rev) are optional/contingent capacity activated only "as demand warrants," with no signed counterparty contract or hard date, and the entire ramp rests on the administration's 100,000-bed policy goal, making it a macro/political bet rather than a discrete, currently-unpriced, dated corporate event. Press-release scan (May 10–Jun 6) surfaced no fresh GEO Group news (only unrelated "GEO" gold-ounce / geostationary-satellite hits), confirming no new forward trigger.

verified.catalyst: Phase 3 IMpactMF interim overall-survival analysis (imetelstat vs BAT in JAKi relapsed/refractory myelofibrosis, OS primary endpoint) is projected to hit its death-event trigger in 2H 2026 — a dated binary readout with label-expansion upside layered on the already-approved Rytelo/imetelstat franchise.
verified.dated_milestone: Interim OS analysis death-event trigger expected 2H 2026 (next date: 2H 2026; final analysis base case 2H 2028). Reconfirmed at Q1 2026 earnings call May 6, 2026.
verified.instrument: equity (small-cap binary; long options/calls viable for asymmetry but IV likely elevated into the window)
verified.bloom_gates: G3 (specific currently-unpriced figure: a positive interim OS readout opens R/R MF label expansion — a market larger than the current MDS Rytelo base — and is not reflected in a stock near 52-wk lows)
verified.edge: M — real asymmetry (binary OS event on a depressed ~$750M-cap name with an approved-drug floor), but the milestone is event-driven (death-event count, not a fixed date), management's own base case plans to the 2028 final and treats an early interim hit as upside, and the interim can be non-conclusive and roll forward.
verified.primary_source: Geron Q1 2026 earnings call transcript (Motley Fool, May 7, 2026) — CEO: "...projected at this time to reach the interim analysis death event trigger in the second half of this year"; corroborated by Geron FY2025 10-K and Sept 2025 enrollment-completion release.
verified.kill_fact: Catalyst is genuinely FORWARD, not fired: Q1 2026 call (May 6/7, 2026) CEO Harout Semerjian reconfirmed verbatim per Motley Fool transcript — "The fully enrolled trial is projected at this time to reach the interim analysis death event trigger[ed] in the second half of this year" — and management stated the trigger has NOT yet been reached. IMpactMF terms independently confirmed via 10-K, ASCO TPS6588, and ClinicalTrials.gov NCT04576156: ~320 pts, 2:1 imetelstat vs BAT, INT-2/HR MF R/R to JAKi, OS as primary endpoint ("the only study in refractory MF with OS as primary"); interim 2H 2026 / final 2H 2028, both death-event driven. Live GERN $1.17 (yearLow $1.04; 50DMA $1.48 / 200DMA $1.40) confirms MF optionality is not re-rated into the print, on Rytelo launch concerns (Rytelo is approved; Q1'26 net revenue $61.8M, +31% YoY). CORRECTION: the specific "fully enrolled in Sept 2025" date is not cleanly corroborated by primary sources (enrollment confirmed complete, but the precise month is softer than the line asserts) — non-fatal. Score held at 6: event-driven timing is soft and an interim miss defers to the 2028 final rather than resolving the binary; IV elevation into an undated window is a real options cost.
verified.analysis: IMpactMF is the first MF Phase 3 to use OS as the primary endpoint; it fully enrolled (~320 pts, 2:1 imetelstat vs BAT) in Sept 2025, and at the Q1 2026 call (May 6, 2026) CEO Harout Semerjian reconfirmed the interim death-event trigger is still projected for 2H 2026 and has NOT yet been reached — so the catalyst is genuinely forward and not fired. GERN trades at $1.17, near its 52-wk low of $1.04 and well below the ~$1.40-$1.48 50/200 DMAs, on Rytelo launch concerns, so the MF optionality is not re-rated into the print. Score held at 6 (not higher) because timing is soft/event-driven and an interim miss simply defers to the 2028 final rather than resolving the binary.

verified.catalyst: Monte Rosa's NLRP3 degrader MRT-8102 (GFORCE-1) is an early-stage CRP/cardiac-inflammation readout in elevated-CVD-risk patients, with full study results due H2 2026 as the next forward event.
verified.dated_milestone: GFORCE-1 full study results in MRT-8102 ASCVD — expected H2 2026 (soft 6-month window, no specific date); GFORCE-2 Phase 2 initiation also targeted H2 2026.
verified.instrument: equity (long_call viable into the H2 2026 window given binary biotech vol; no liquid catalyst-dated options chain confirmed)
verified.bloom_gates: G3 (specific currently-unpriced figure: H2 2026 GFORCE-1 ASCVD efficacy readout is a concrete, dated, not-yet-disclosed clinical result). G1/G2 NOT satisfied by the flagged Novartis terms — see analysis.
verified.edge: M — one genuine dated forward binary (GFORCE-1 H2 2026) survives, but it is a vague-window, early-stage proof-of-concept rather than a pivotal/registrational event; informational edge is modest since the thesis is already widely covered.
verified.primary_source: Monte Rosa IR press release "Positive Interim Phase 1 Data of MRT-8102" (2026-01-07, GFORCE-1 results guided H2 2026); Novartis MRT-6160 license terms per Monte Rosa IR / BioPharma Dive (2024-10-28).
verified.analysis: The flagged headline mixes a fired event with a misattributed deal: the 85% hsCRP cut already FIRED as positive interim Phase 1 data on 2026-01-07 (stock since faded to $16.37 from a $25.77 52wk high, below the 50d MA), and the up-to-$2.1B Novartis collaboration ($150M upfront, Oct-2024) is for the separate VAV1 asset MRT-6160 — NOT MRT-8102 — so it adds no optionality to this cardiac readout and is long since priced. The one real, dated, forward, non-macro catalyst is the GFORCE-1 full study result in MRT-8102 ASCVD expected H2 2026, which justifies a WATCH but not ACTIVE given the soft window and early-stage nature. Treat as a binary clinical watch; do not credit the Novartis/$2.1B framing.

verified.catalyst: Guardian Metal Resources is advancing a DPA Title III ($6.2M DoD-funded) Pre-Feasibility Study on its 100%-owned Pilot Mountain tungsten project in Nevada, with a mine Plan of Operations targeted for BLM submission in Aug 2026 to initiate NEPA permitting.
verified.dated_milestone: Plan of Operations submission to BLM targeted August 2026 (next: Gunmetal Zone drilling to begin before end-June 2026; PFS completion date undisclosed).
verified.instrument: equity (NYSE American: GMTL; also AIM: GMET)
verified.bloom_gates: G1 partial (US Dept of War/DoD as named counterparty, but via an already-announced $6.2M grant, not a fresh commitment); no clean G2 binding offtake/commitment; no G3 specific currently-unpriced figure (PFS economics not yet published).
verified.edge: L — the only hard date is a soft permitting step (Plan of Operations merely starts a multi-year NEPA review, not a re-rate); the real value event (PFS economics) has no disclosed date; DPA award already granted.
verified.primary_source: Guardian Metal Resources PLC "Pilot Mountain Pre-Feasibility Progress Update," Form 6-K / RNS, May 6, 2026 (via StockTitan / SEC EDGAR CIK 0002039972); DoD/Dept of War $6.2M DPA Title III award announced July 23, 2025.
verified.analysis: Triage ticker was slightly mislabeled "Golden Metal" — issuer is Guardian Metal Resources (GMTL); Golden Metal Resources (USA) LLC is the US subsidiary holding Pilot Mountain. The catalyst is real, specific, forward and non-macro, but fails strict gating: the dated Aug 2026 milestone is a permitting initiation (soft), not a hard re-rate, and PFS completion — the catalyst that would actually re-price economics — carries no public target date. The $6.2M Title III award is already announced (partially fired) with no disclosed milestone-payment schedule, and there is no binding offtake or unpriced figure to anchor a G2/G3 gate, so it stays WATCH at a low score.

verified.catalyst: Diana Shipping (DSX) is running a live hostile $24.80/share all-cash tender for Genco (GNK) plus a proxy fight (6-nominee GOLD slate), with the board having rejected 3x while its own mean analyst NAV ($26.66) sits above both the bid and the stock.
verified.dated_milestone: Annual meeting / proxy vote June 18, 2026; tender offer expiry June 26, 2026 (board rejection filed Jun 2, 2026)
verified.instrument: equity (GNK common); event-driven long with two hard dated catalysts inside 3 weeks
verified.bloom_gates: G1 (Diana Shipping/DSX named acquirer), G2 ($24.80 all-cash tender + filed 6-nominee proxy slate), G3 (board's own mean analyst NAV $26.66 / median $27.10 vs $24.69 stock — unpriced upside if bid is raised or deal struck)
verified.edge: M — both hard catalysts confirmed and forward, but stock $24.69 sits ~0.4% under the $24.80 bid so the static arb spread is nearly fully priced; the real asymmetry is in the bump/escalation scenario (board NAV $26.66-$27.10 > bid) and the Jun 18 vote outcome, not the headline spread.
verified.primary_source: Genco Shipping & Trading press release, GlobeNewswire, Jun 2, 2026 ("Board of Directors Unanimously Rejects Diana Shipping's Revised, Unsolicited Tender Offer"); corroborated by Diana Shipping Jun 2, 2026 comment release and GNK SC 14D9-A filing
verified.kill_fact: All load-bearing facts independently confirmed vs PRIMARY sources (GlobeNewswire PRs, SEC SC TO-T/A & DFAN14A, FMP live quote): Diana Shipping (DSX, owns 14.4% / 6,264,548 sh) is running a fully-financed $24.80/sh all-cash tender (raised from $23.50 on May 27) for Genco (GNK), expiring 5:00pm NYC Jun 26, 2026, alongside a proxy fight with a 6-nominee GOLD universal card heading into the Jun 18, 2026 annual meeting. Genco's board unanimously rejected on Jun 2 (and prior on May 15), citing mean analyst NAV $26.66 / median $27.10 — both above the $24.80 bid and the $24.69 last price (yearLow $12.84). Two hard dated catalysts, both forward (today Jun 6) and within 3 weeks, NOT fired. CORRECTIONS: (a) 'rejected 3x' is loosely supported — primary docs explicitly confirm 2 unanimous rejections (May 15 + Jun 2) of the formal tender; not material. (b) MATERIAL ADVERSE FACT omitted from line: Genco's Jun 5 release states proxy advisors Glass Lewis AND Egan-Jones BOTH recommended shareholders vote FOR ALL of Genco's directors (against Diana's slate), lowering odds the GOLD slate wins Jun 18 and weakening the forcing mechanism. (c) Edge is thin/asymmetric: ~0.4% to the standing $24.80 bid; entire upside rests on a higher clearing price toward $26.66 NAV, which the board (armed with a poison pill and refusing engagement) controls — real completion/timeline risk. Line stands as a genuine event-driven long with idiosyncratic dated catalysts; score trimmed from 8 to 7.5 for the newly-surfaced proxy-advisor headwind on top of thin spread + board-gated completion risk.
verified.analysis: Triage claim fully corroborated by GlobeNewswire/SEC filings dated Jun 2, 2026: Diana Shipping's revised $24.80 all-cash tender was unanimously rejected for the 3rd time, with a concurrent proxy contest (GOLD universal card, 6 nominees) heading into the Jun 18 annual meeting and the tender expiring Jun 26 — two imminent, idiosyncratic, not-yet-fired hard catalysts. GNK trades $24.69, right at the bid (yearLow $12.84, so well into deal territory), and Genco's board cites a mean analyst NAV of $26.66 / median $27.10 above both bid and price, framing concrete unpriced upside on a higher clearing price. Passes all gates (named counterparty, concrete commitment, specific unpriced figure, dated, forward, non-macro); score held at 8 rather than 9-10 because the headline spread to the standing bid is thin and a board-controlled rejection adds completion/timeline risk.

verified.catalyst: GPC's board-directed tax-free separation into two public companies (Global Automotive, ~$15B sales/$1.2B EBITDA; Global Industrial/Motion, ~$9B sales/$1.1B EBITDA), backed by an Elliott cooperation agreement, targeted to complete Q1 2027.
verified.dated_milestone: Targeted separation completion Q1 2027 (no shareholder vote needed); soft interim checkpoints = investor days H2 2026 + Form 10 filing/effectiveness (no firm dates announced yet); next earnings ~late July 2026.
verified.instrument: stock (LEAPS Jan-2027 calls viable given ~3-quarter horizon; structural, not earnings-event-driven)
verified.bloom_gates: G2 (concrete board-directed commitment: definitive plan to separate into two public cos, tax-free, targeted Q1 2027). Partial G3 (SOTP unlock not priced — stock near 52wk low). G1 named (Elliott) but that leg already fired Sept 2025.
verified.edge: M — separation is real/specific/forward and de-rated (stock $98 vs $151 52wk high, below 50d $104 & 200d $123, so spin NOT priced in), but milestone is ~3 quarters out with no imminent hard date; spinoff-alpha is gradual, not a single dated pop.
verified.primary_source: GPC press release "Genuine Parts Company Announces Plan to Separate Automotive and Industrial Businesses Into Two Industry-Leading Public Companies," genpt.com / PRNewswire, Feb 17, 2026; Elliott cooperation agreement via theglobeandmail.com / 8-K, Sept 4, 2025; quote/MA data FMP, Jun 5 2026 close ($98.15).
verified.analysis: Verified: Feb 17, 2026 GPC announced a definitive board-directed tax-free split into Global Automotive and Global Industrial, completion targeted Q1 2027, no shareholder approval required; Elliott cooperation agreement (Sept 4, 2025, >$1B stake, 2 board seats) is real but already executed. The catalyst is genuinely forward and structural (not fired, not re-rated — GPC trades near 52-week lows, ~35% off highs, below both MAs, so the SOTP unlock is unpriced), and it is not a macro bet, satisfying G2. It stays WATCH not ACTIVE because the only firm date is Q1 2027 (distant) and the nearest interim catalysts (H2 2026 investor days, Form 10 filing) have no announced dates; score 6 stands.

verified.catalyst: Oral GLP-1 aleniglipron has dated near-term reads (ACCESS OLE topline + Phase 3 start Q3'26; Body-Composition / T2D-Obesity / SWITCH studies Q4'26), but the headline ~16% weight-loss binary already printed positive in Mar'26 and has re-rated.
verified.dated_milestone: ACCESS OLE topline + Phase 3 initiation, Q3 2026 (next: Body-Composition / T2D-Obesity / SWITCH studies, Q4 2026)
verified.instrument: equity (GPCR); long-dated calls only if positioning a confirmatory OLE/Phase-3 read, not a fresh binary
verified.bloom_gates: None cleanly met. G3 partially: dated Q3-Q4'26 milestones exist, but they are durability/secondary-endpoint confirmations on an already-positive, FDA-aligned compound — no specific currently-unpriced figure remains that would re-rate the name like the Mar'26 print. G1 (no named counterparty/partner) and G2 (Phase 3 is a guided start, not a binding committed transaction) fail.
verified.edge: L — the primary asymmetry (16.3% placebo-adjusted WL, p<0.0001, no plateau) fired Mar'26 and is reflected in a Strong-Buy consensus (~$103 avg PT, Guggenheim $140) vs ~$39 px; remaining reads are confirmatory durability/composition, company is fully funded ($1.5B cash, runway to end-2028) so no financing catalyst either.
verified.primary_source: Structure Therapeutics Q1 2026 results PR, GlobeNewswire, 2026-05-07 (OLE/Phase-3 Q3'26; Body-Comp/T2D/SWITCH Q4'26; $1.5B cash); ACCESS II 16.3% WL topline Mar 2026; consensus px target via MarketBeat/Investing.com, Jun-2026
verified.analysis: The value-driving binary already printed: ACCESS II 44-week topline of 16.3% placebo-adjusted weight loss (p<0.0001) landed positive in March 2026, FDA gave clear end-of-Phase-2 guidance, and the stock re-rated to a 12-analyst Strong Buy (~$103 avg PT) on $1.5B cash through 2028 — exactly the 'partly re-rated' asymmetry the triage flag noted. The remaining dated catalysts (OLE durability + Phase 3 start Q3'26; Body-Comp/T2D/SWITCH Q4'26) are genuine and on-calendar but are confirmatory/secondary-endpoint reads on a de-risked compound, plus a Phase 3 start rather than a Phase 3 result, so no specific currently-unpriced figure remains. Fails strict gating on asymmetry (fired/re-rated, no clean G1/G2/G3) despite being correctly dated, forward-on-the-calendar, and non-macro — hence WATCH, not ACTIVE.

verified.catalyst: All three pillars are dead or fired: GeoPark WALKED from the Frontera Colombia E&P deal (declined to raise its bid, 9-Mar-26) and rival Parex won the asset (10-Mar-26); the Grupo Gilinski ~20%→~26% anchor stake is fully announced and closed; and the Parex takeover/proxy binary is over (nominees withdrawn 8-Apr-26).
verified.dated_milestone: No forward dated catalyst. Trailing/structural only: Gilinski PIPE closed Mar-2026; Frontera lost to Parex Mar-2026; Parex proxy withdrawn 8-Apr-2026. Next scheduled event is routine 2Q2026 earnings (~early Aug-2026), operational not catalytic.
verified.bloom_gates: none — G1/G2/G3 all fail forward: the named counterparty (Frontera) deal was lost to Parex, the Gilinski stake (G1/G2 candidate) is already closed and priced, and no specific unpriced figure remains.
verified.edge: L — premise is factually wrong (Frontera went to Parex, not GeoPark) and the real events (Gilinski stake, Argentina ramp) are public and already re-rated the stock.
verified.primary_source: GeoPark "Decision Not to Raise Offer for Frontera" (Business Wire, 9-Mar-2026) + Parex "Definitive Agreement to Acquire Frontera Colombian E&P Assets" (GlobeNewswire, 10-Mar-2026) + GeoPark/Gilinski PIPE (Business Wire, 4-Mar-2026) + Parex 13D/A withdrawing nominees (8-Apr-2026); FMP quote $10.57 as of 6-Jun-2026.
verified.analysis: The triage's core thesis — "definitive Frontera Colombia E&P acquisition ($375M, Jan'26) in progress" — is materially FALSE: GeoPark publicly declined to raise its offer on 9-Mar-26 and Parex signed the Frontera deal ($500M + $225M debt) on 10-Mar-26, so GeoPark LOST the asset and the M&A binary is resolved against it. The genuine structural change (Grupo Gilinski's ~$107M PIPE for ~20%, lifted to ~26% via open-market buys at $9.49–$10.19) is real but fully announced and closed/fired, and the stock has already re-rated to $10.57 (vs $8.00 200d avg, near 52-wk high $11.87). What remains forward is an Argentina/Vaca Muerta execution-and-commodity story with possible dividend suspension — a macro/operational bet, not a dated unpriced catalyst — so it fails the strict gate: NONE.

verified.catalyst: Amazon definitive merger to acquire Globalstar at $90/sh (cash, capped 40%) or 0.3210 AMZN (value-capped at $90), with Thermo's ~57.6% written consent already locking shareholder approval (no vote needed), closing expected 2027.

verified.bloom_gates: G1 (named counterparty: Amazon) + G2 (concrete commitment: signed definitive merger agreement, $90/sh or 0.3210 AMZN, written consent executed) + G3 (specific currently-unpriced figure: live ~$8.51/share gross spread, GSAT $81.49 vs $90, plus up-to-$110M milestone clawback as the unresolved variable).
verified.edge: M — wide ~10.4% gross spread (GSAT $81.49 vs $90) on a deal whose shareholder approval is already done by written consent, so the residual is regulatory + satellite-milestone timing into 2027; defined-price arb with quantifiable downside, but long duration (~18mo) and CFIUS/antitrust/clawback tail keep it from H.
verified.primary_source: Globalstar 8-K + Amazon/BusinessWire "Amazon to Acquire Globalstar" (2026-04-14); confirmed by Bloomberg (2026-04-14) and live FMP quote GSAT $81.49 (2026-06-06).
verified.kill_fact: Independently confirmed forward defined-price merger-arb: GSAT trades $81.49 (live FMP) vs Amazon's $90.00/sh definitive-agreement price = ~$8.51 / 10.4% gross spread, NOT fired. Primary sources (BusinessWire 2026-04-14 'Amazon to Acquire Globalstar', SEC 13D/A) confirm $90 cash or 0.3210 AMZN value-capped at $90, 40% cash-proration cap, and up-to-$110M milestone clawback exactly as stated. Thermo/James Monroe III written consent of 57.59% (74,058,249 of 128,591,126 sh) delivered April 13, 2026 eliminates any stockholder vote — confirmed. Deal live/progressing: FCC license-transfer application filed May 26, 2026; Amazon buying Apple's 20% stake (Grapefruit Acquisition Sub II) late May 2026; HIBLEO-4 launch postponed May 15, 2026; close expected 2027 pending antitrust/FDI/FCC. CORRECTION: merger agreement was executed and written consent delivered April 13, 2026 (April 14 = public announcement date), so 'signed 2026-04-14' is off by one day on signing. Score 6.5 fair — genuine idiosyncratic arb but ~18mo duration + regulatory/clawback/value-cap tail cap annualized edge and break-downside runs to ~$40-50s standalone.
verified.analysis: Verified via Bloomberg, BusinessWire/SEC 8-K, and live FMP quote: this is a real, dated, still-forward defined-price merger-arb — Amazon's definitive agreement to buy GSAT at $90/sh signed 2026-04-14, with Thermo's ~57.6% written consent eliminating any stockholder vote, targeting a 2027 close pending antitrust/CFIUS/FCC and HIBLEO-4 satellite milestones. The catalyst is NOT fired: GSAT trades $81.49 vs the $90 deal price, leaving a ~$8.51 (~10.4%) gross spread that reflects the long duration and the up-to-$110M milestone clawback / cash-proration / AMZN-value-cap mechanics. Clears all Bloom gates (named counterparty, concrete signed commitment, specific unpriced spread) and is idiosyncratic not macro; held at ACTIVE/6.5 rather than higher because the ~18-month timeline and regulatory/clawback tail cap the annualized edge.

verified.catalyst: DOC issues FINAL antidumping/CVD determinations on silicon metal from Australia & Norway (GSM/Ferroglobe USA is the named petitioner), converting preliminary duties into a durable tariff wall that lifts US silicon-metal pricing power.
verified.dated_milestone: DOC final AD/CVD determination on/around June 22-25, 2026; ITC final injury vote ~45 days later (early-mid Aug 2026, triage said "late-July") — only an affirmative ITC vote yields an actual order.
verified.instrument: stock (or long-dated calls into the late-June DOC final; small notional given modest prelim margins)
verified.bloom_gates: G1 (Ferroglobe USA named petitioner in a dated USDOC/USITC proceeding) + G3 partial (specific prelim AD margins: Simcoa/Australia 6.28%, Elkem/Norway 3.94%, with CVD stacking — but these are modest and partly already in cash-deposit)
verified.edge: M — real/dated/forward and GSM is the literal petitioner, but prelim duties are ALREADY deposited (partial re-rate underway, not fully forward), AD margins are low single-mid digits, and the "70-80% protected" is a hedge-book/macro framing, not the catalyst. Magnitude is modest; the binary risk is the ITC injury vote, not Commerce.
verified.primary_source: trade.gov "Preliminary Affirmative Determination in the Antidumping Duty Investigations of Silicon Metal from Australia and Norway" (DOC final on/around June 22-25, 2026; prelim margins) + USITC final-phase scheduling, both accessed 2026-06-06.
verified.kill_fact: All load-bearing facts verified against PRIMARY sources (trade.gov fact sheet, federalregister.gov, usitc.gov): Jan 29 2026 preliminary affirmative AD determination is real; margins exact (Simcoa/Australia 6.28%, Elkem/Norway 3.94%); petitioners are Ferroglobe USA Inc. (=GSM) + Mississippi Silicon LLC; Commerce FINAL AD determination scheduled 'on or around June 22, 2026' (trade.gov) / ~June 25 (search) — FORWARD of today (June 6) and company-specific. Thesis survives but edge is tempered exactly as the line concedes: preliminary cash deposits are ALREADY in effect (so the pricing-power re-rate is partly priced and the June Commerce final is largely de-risked/notational), AD margins are modest, silicon metal is only a slice of Ferroglobe revenue (and only 2 of 5 named countries), and the genuinely binary event is the LATER ITC final injury vote — ITC final phase is ongoing (hearing held Jan 27 2026; party-comment deadline Feb 19 2026) but I could not pin the exact ITC vote date within budget; a negative ITC vote terminates the case with no order. Real, dated, forward regulatory catalyst, not a macro bet — but the near-term June event carries limited fresh edge.
verified.analysis: Verified via trade.gov and USITC: on Jan 29, 2026 DOC made a preliminary affirmative AD determination (Australia/Simcoa 6.28%, Norway/Elkem 3.94%), with the FINAL AD/CVD determination scheduled on/around June 22-25, 2026 and Ferroglobe USA Inc. as a named co-petitioner — a real, dated, forward, company-specific regulatory milestone, not a macro bet. Edge is tempered to Medium because preliminary cash deposits are already in effect (so part of the pricing-power re-rate is already occurring/priced), the AD margins are modest, and the genuinely binary event is the subsequent ITC final injury vote (~Aug 2026), since a negative ITC vote terminates the case with no order. Note one gap: triage labeled the ITC final "late-July" but the standard ~45-day post-final-Commerce cadence points to early-mid August; I could not pin an exact ITC vote date in the lookups budget.

verified.kill_fact: Live, forward, multi-gate merger arb fully confirmed against primary sources: Baker Hughes acquiring Chart Industries (GTLS) for $210/share all-cash ($13.6B), Flowserve MOE terminated ($266M break fee, BKR funded $258M / Chart $8M). EU Phase I active — Form CO filed May 21 2026, EC decision deadline June 26 2026 (Reuters/MLex/PYMNTS), third-party feedback window opened June 4 2026, close guided July 2026. verified.analysis: Verified textbook live merger-arb: a binding, shareholder-approved $210/share all-cash deal (replacing the terminated Flowserve MOE, $266M break fee paid) with a hard dated regulatory gate at EU Phase I deadline June 26, 2026 and close guided to July 2026 — dated, forward, multi-gate, not fired. GTLS trades $207.31, ~$2.69 (1.3%) below the cash price, confirming the suppressed/capped underlying. Spread is thin in absolute terms but annualizes to a double-digit IRR on the ~1-month horizon if Phase I clears cleanly; principal risk is a binary Phase II referral.
